choice: Carry the spreadsheet's annual real spending schedule as the baseline (inflation-adjusted each month via the stochastic CPI we already draw), expressed as a real-dollar target. Support a single spending stream for v1, with the data shape allowing phased spending (go-go/slow-go/no-go) and discretionary-vs-essential splits to be layered on later.
rationale: Spending demand is the dominant driver of depletion risk, so it must be explicit. The spreadsheet already encodes a real spending path; reusing it keeps fidelity, and tying it to the stochastic inflation block (fe17d0a2) keeps real-terms spending coherent with returns. Phases are a known ProjectionLab/BoldIn capability but add scope — defer the modeling, keep the data shape open.
